paras.text: College ke notice board pe ek naya notice chipka tha, itni ajeeb formal language me likha tha ki {{A}} aur {{B}} dono ruk gaye padhne ke liye.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (zor se padhte hue, mazak udate hue) 'It is imperative that every student BE present in the auditorium by 9 AM sharp.' Ye 'BE' kya hai yahan? 'is' kyu nahi likha?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke) Arre ye galti nahi hai, ye ek KHAAS grammar form hai — Present Subjunctive kehte hain isko! Bahut official/formal notices, rules, aur demands me use hota hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Present Subjunctive? Naam se hi darr lag raha hai. Samjhao na, kya scene hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul simple hai, dekhte hi dekhte samajh aa jayega! Jab kisi cheez ko bahut ZAROORI, DEMAND, ya FORMAL SUGGESTION ki tarah bola jaata hai, tab uske 'that' clause ke andar wala verb hamesha BASE FORM me rehta hai — chahe subject 'he/she/it' ho, chahe sentence present ho ya past, koi fark nahi padta.It is imperative that every student be present in the auditorium by 9 AM Dekho — 'every student' third-person singular hai, normal grammar me 'is' aata, but yahan 'BE' hi rehta hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hairan) Ye to bilkul ulta lagta hai jo humne pehle seekha tha! To ye sirf notice-board wali cheez hai, ya roz ki English me bhi hota hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Roz ki spoken English me kam hota hai, but FORMAL writing — notices, rules, official letters, meeting minutes, exam answers — inme bahut common hai. Aur SSC me to ye favorite error-spotting topic hai, kyunki 90% log 'is/was/are' likh dete hain galti se.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ek cheez samajh nahi aayi — 'It is necessary that everyone DOES his work efficiently' — ye galat kyu hai? Sunne me to sahi lagta hai, 'everyone' ke saath 'does' hi to aata hai normally!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bahut hi important sawaal, isi se poora subjunctive samajh aata hai! Dhyan se socho — 'everyone DOES his work efficiently' ka matlab kya hoga agar ye sach me sahi hota? Iska matlab hoga — ye ek FACT hai, ki sabhi log ALREADY apna kaam efficiently karte hain, ye unka roz ka habit/reality hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sochte hue) Haan, matlab wahi jo ho raha hai already.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul! Ab socho, agar sach me sab efficiently kaam kar hi rahe hain, to 'It is necessary that...' bolne ki zarurat hi kya hai? Jo pehle se ho raha hai, uske liye 'zaroori hai' bolna hi ajeeb hai! Yehi to point hai — 'It is necessary' ka matlab hai ye abhi NAHI ho raha, ya pakka nahi hai ki hoga, isliye hum ye DEMAND/REQUIREMENT bata rahe hain ki ye HONA CHAHIYE — jo abhi reality nahi hai, ek expectation hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (aankhein badi karte hue) Ohhh! Ab samajh aaya — 'DOES' se lagta hai ye already sach hai, 'FACT' hai. But hum to REQUIREMENT bata rahe hain, kuch jo HONA CHAHIYE, abhi guaranteed nahi hai. Isliye 'does' galat hai, 'DO' (base form) sahi hai, kyunki wo requirement/wish dikhata hai, fact nahi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Ekdum perfect samjhe tumne! Yehi hai subjunctive ka asli logic — BASE FORM use karke hum batate hain 'ye abhi sach nahi hai, but ye HONA CHAHIYE/ZAROORI hai.' Agar hum 'is/does/are' jaisa NORMAL (Indicative Mood) form use karte, to matlab hota 'ye already sach hai' — jo sense hi nahi banata jab tum 'necessary/essential/demand' jaisa word use kar rahe ho.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ek aur example do na isi tarah, taaki pakka clear ho jaaye ye logic.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — socho 'It is necessary that he TAKES rest' vs 'It is necessary that he TAKE rest'. Pehla wala ('takes') ka matlab — 'wo already rest leta hai' (fact/habit) — to phir 'necessary' bolne ka koi matlab nahi, jo already ho raha hai! Doosra wala ('take', base form) ka matlab — 'abhi rest nahi le raha, but usko lena CHAHIYE' — yehi sahi sense hai jab hum 'necessary' bol rahe hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (pakka likhte hue) Wah, ab ye trick hamesha yaad rahegi — jab bhi 'necessary/essential/suggest/demand' jaisa word dikhe, sochna hai 'ye abhi TRUE nahi hai, sirf ek REQUIREMENT/WISH hai' — isliye BASE FORM, normal 's' wala fact-form nahi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi trick! Ek aur cheez batani hai isi ke sath related — jab that-clause PASSIVE VOICE me ho, tab kya hota hai? Kabhi socha hai?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Passive matlab jaise 'kaam kiya jaata hai' wala? Nahi socha, samjhao.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: It is necessary that the work be done efficiently — dekho, passive me bhi wahi logic hai — 'BE' base form hai (jaise 'is' ka base form 'be' hota hai), phir V3 (past participle) aata hai. 'is done' ya 'was done' kabhi nahi, hamesha 'BE done', 'BE submitted', 'BE reopened'.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, matlab active wale me base-form verb ('take', 'come', 'sign'), aur passive wale me 'BE + V3' ('be done', 'be submitted') — dono me ROOT logic same hai, bas passive me ek extra 'BE' aata hai V3 ke pehle?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi samjhe! Chalo isi original example ko active aur passive dono me bana ke dikhata hoon, taaki fark bilkul clear ho jaaye. Active: 'It is necessary that everyone DO his work efficiently.' (subject khud kaam kar raha hai) Passive: 'It is necessary that the work BE DONE efficiently (by everyone).' (kaam pe focus hai, kisne kiya uspe nahi)

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me dono side-by-side likhte hue) Ab bilkul clear hai — active me base-form verb seedha, passive me 'be' + V3. Ek aur passive example do na, kisi doosre trigger word ke sath.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — 'demand' ke sath: 'The workers demanded that their salary BE INCREASED.' (na ki 'is increased' ya 'was increased'). Aur 'insist' ke sath: 'The teacher insisted that the mistake BE CORRECTED immediately.' (na ki 'is corrected').

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Perfect, ab poora logic aur passive dono clear ho gaye. Isse mujhe SSC ke error-spotting me bahut help milegi, kyunki ab main sirf ratt nahi raha, samajh raha hoon KYU 's' nahi lagta.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, chalo poora samjhao — kaunse words ke baad ye BASE FORM wala rule lagta hai? Poori list chahiye mujhe.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo, do groups me yaad rakho. Pehla group — ADJECTIVES, jo 'It is + adjective + that' pattern me aate hain: essential, necessary, important, imperative, vital, crucial, desirable, urgent, obligatory, advisable. Doosra group — VERBS jo seedhe use hote hain: suggest, recommend, demand, insist, propose, require, urge, request, order, move, desire, prefer.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook nikaal ke) Itni lambi list yaad kaise rahegi?! Chalo ek-ek karke maza wala example do, taaki yaad reh jaaye.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Perfect idea, chalo masti karte hain isi notice board se! Dekho agla notice — 'It is essential that all bikes be parked outside the gate.' Isko funny bana ke socho — jaise principal khud bike lekar andar ghusne wale students ko pakadta hoga.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Haha, achha imagine kar liya! Ab ek aur do, 'imperative' wala.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — imagine karo hostel warden itni strict hai ki roz chillati hai — 'It is imperative that everyone return before 9 PM!' Har ek student darr ke maare bhaagta hoga gate band hone se pehle.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Bilkul apni warden jaisi lag rahi hai! Ab 'vital' wala do.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — cricket coach bol raha hai team se match se pehle — 'It is vital that every player arrive at the ground by 6 AM, or you're benched!' Socho, coach ka gussa waala face.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Ye to bilkul movie scene jaisa lag raha hai! 'Crucial' wala bhi do.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — exam ke time principal announcement karti hai — 'It is crucial that every candidate carry their admit card, or they will be denied entry!' Imagine karo ek bechara student gate pe rota hua khada hai, admit card ghar bhool aaya.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Bechara! Achha ab 'desirable' aur 'urgent' bhi karo — inme kya fark hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'Desirable' thoda soft hota hai, matlab 'better hoga agar' — 'It is desirable that students wear the uniform on Mondays.' (halki suggestion, majboori itni strict nahi). 'Urgent' matlab bahut jaldi zaroori — 'It is urgent that the injured player receive medical help immediately!' (turant, koi delay nahi).

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me likhte hue) Achha, fark clear ho gaya — 'desirable' soft advice, 'urgent' turant zaroori. Ab last adjective wala bhi karo — 'obligatory'.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: 'Obligatory' matlab koi choice hi nahi, rule hai — jaise 'It is obligatory that every citizen carry a valid ID during elections.' Government wale notices me bahut common hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ab VERBS wala group karte hain — sabse pehle 'suggest' aur 'recommend', ye dono kaafi same lagte hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haan, dono halke advice jaise hain. Chalo funny example — imagine karo, tumhari mummy bol rahi hai doctor ko — 'I suggest that my son EAT more vegetables!' Aur doctor turant bolta hai — 'I recommend that he EXERCISE daily too!' Dono me base form — 'eat', 'exercise', 's' nahi laga.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Bilkul meri mummy jaisi lag rahi hai! Ab 'demand' aur 'insist' — ye to bahut strong lagte hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul strong hain! Imagine karo — angry customer restaurant me chilla raha hai — 'I demand that the manager REFUND my money right now!' Aur manager darr ke bolta hai — 'I insist that you TAKE a complimentary dessert instead, sir!'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (zor se hasm ke) Ye scene to bilkul real lag raha hai, maine dekha hai aisa kabhi restaurant me! Ab 'propose' aur 'require' bhi karo.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo — school ke meeting me teacher bol rahi hai — 'I propose that the annual function BE held in December this year.' Aur rule book bolta hai — 'The exam rules require that every student SIGN the attendance sheet.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, ab 'urge', 'request', aur 'order' bhi karo — ye teeno me kya fark hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Teeno me STRENGTH ka fark hai — 'urge' matlab bahut zor de ke kehna (jaise doctor bimaar patient se) — 'The doctor urged that the patient REST for a week.' 'request' matlab politely poochna — 'She requested that the deadline BE extended by two days.' 'order' matlab seedha hukum, authority wala — 'The general ordered that the troops MOVE immediately.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me sab likhte hue) Achha aakhri do bache hain — 'desire' aur 'prefer', aur wo 'wants that' wala jo roz sunte hain.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo, formal wale pehle — 'desire' aur 'prefer' bahut formal writing me aate hain, jaise official documents — 'The company desires that all employees ARRIVE by 9 AM.' 'The board prefers that the meeting BE held online.' Aur roz ki spoken English wala, jo bahut common hai — 'The boss wants that everybody COME on time.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hairan) Arre ye to maine kal hi bola tha apni team se, bina jaane subjunctive use kar liya!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke) Dekha! Ye sab tumhare roz ki zindagi me already ghumti hai, bas naam nahi pata tha. Waise ek zaroori baat — 'wants THAT' pattern bhi chalta hai but 'wants everyone TO come' (infinitive wala) zyada common/casual hai roz ki baat-cheet me. Exam me dono type aa sakte hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ek doubt hai — agar sentence PAST me shuru ho, jaise 'The teacher suggested...' — tab bhi base form hi rahega, past nahi hoga?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bahut achha sawaal, ye sabse zyada log galat karte hain! Haan, chahe reporting verb PAST me ho ('suggested', 'demanded', 'insisted'), that-clause ka verb PHIR BHI base form hi rahega — kabhi past nahi hota subjunctive me. 'The teacher suggested that he BE more careful' — 'was' ya 'is' nahi, hamesha 'be'.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (pakka likhte hue) Ye bahut important hai, kyunki natural instinct to past lagane ka hi hota hai jab sentence past me ho.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi, yehi to SSC ka trap hai! Chalo ab poora masti wala practice round karte hain — main tumhe funny scenarios dunga, tum subjunctive sentence banao.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Chalo, ready hoon, laao!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Pehla — imagine karo tumhari class teacher bahut gussa hai kyunki koi roz late aata hai. Wo notice lagati hai — use 'imperative' word se banao.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sochte hue, hasm ke) 'It is imperative that every student arrive before the bell rings!'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect! Doosra — imagine ek pizza shop me, waiter bahut slow hai, angry customer bol raha hai — 'demand' word se banao.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'I demand that the manager serve my order immediately!'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haha ekdum sahi feel aaya! Teesra — coach team se bol raha hai match se pehle, 'insist' word se.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: 'The coach insisted that every player practice for two extra hours.'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Perfect! Chautha — tumhare papa ka favorite dialogue, 'necessary' word se, tumhari padhai ke baare me.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) 'It is necessary that I study for at least four hours every day!' Bilkul mere papa ki awaaz me suna maine ye!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (zor se hasm ke) Perfect, full marks! Ab tum sach me expert ho gaye ho — kisi bhi trigger word (adjective ya verb) ko dekhte hi turant pehchan sakte ho ki base form aayega.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Sach me, ab wo notice board bhi mujhe funny lagega, har baar 'be/come/attend' dekhoonga to yaad aayega humari ye masti bhari class.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Bas ek promise karo — 'It is essential that you revise this topic before the exam!' — dekha, main bhi khud subjunctive use kar rahi hoon tumpe order chalane ke liye!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (salute karte hue, hasm ke) Yes madam, it is obligatory that I obey!

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'It is imperative that everybody comes on time.'
quiz.answer: It is imperative that everybody come on time.
quiz.explain: 'Imperative' triggers the Present Subjunctive — the verb after 'that' stays in base form ('come'), never takes 's', even with a word like 'everybody' that feels singular.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The boss wants that everybody comes on time.'
quiz.answer: The boss wants that everybody come on time.
quiz.explain: 'Wants that' is a valid (though less formal) Present Subjunctive trigger — same rule applies: base form after 'that', no 's'.

quiz.q: Fill (adjective trigger, meaning 'very important'): 'It is ___ that she attend the interview tomorrow.'
quiz.answer: vital / crucial / essential / important / imperative (any of these fit)
quiz.explain: All these adjectives trigger the same pattern: It is + adjective + that + subject + base form.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The rule requires that every member signs the form.'
quiz.answer: The rule requires that every member sign the form.
quiz.explain: 'Require' is a verb trigger for the Present Subjunctive — base form 'sign', not 'signs', even though 'every member' is singular.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The teacher suggested that he was more careful in future.'
quiz.answer: The teacher suggested that he be more careful in future.
quiz.explain: Even when the reporting verb ('suggested') is in the past tense, the that-clause verb STAYS in base form — it never becomes past tense in the Present Subjunctive.

quiz.q: Fill (verb trigger, strong demand): 'The angry customer ___ that the manager refund his money.'
quiz.explain: 'Demand/demanded' is a classic Present Subjunctive trigger verb — the following clause takes base form ('refund'), regardless of the tense of 'demand' itself.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'It is necessary that the report is submitted by Friday.'
quiz.answer: It is necessary that the report be submitted by Friday.
quiz.explain: 'Necessary' triggers Present Subjunctive — passive voice inside the that-clause also uses base form 'be' (not 'is'), so 'be submitted' is correct.

quiz.q: Which word does NOT trigger the Present Subjunctive pattern: suggest / recommend / believe / insist?
quiz.explain: 'Believe' reports an opinion/fact, not a demand or formal suggestion — it doesn't trigger the subjunctive. Suggest/recommend/insist all do.

quiz.q: Explain in one line: why is 'It is necessary that everyone does his work efficiently' WRONG?
quiz.answer: 'Does' (Indicative) states this is already happening as a fact/habit — but 'It is necessary' means it's NOT guaranteed yet, it's a requirement. The correct form is base form 'do', which expresses a requirement, not a fact.
quiz.explain: Subjunctive base form signals 'this should happen, not that it already does' — using the normal 's' form contradicts the very meaning of 'necessary/essential/demand'.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'It is necessary that the work is done efficiently.'
quiz.answer: It is necessary that the work be done efficiently.
quiz.explain: Passive Present Subjunctive uses 'BE + V3' (base form 'be', not 'is'), following the same base-form logic as the active voice.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The workers demanded that their salary is increased.'
quiz.answer: The workers demanded that their salary be increased.
quiz.explain: 'Demand' triggers Present Subjunctive — in passive voice, this becomes 'be + V3' ('be increased'), never 'is/was increased'.

quiz.q: Fill (passive subjunctive): 'The committee insisted that the decision ___ (reconsider) before the vote.'
quiz.explain: Passive subjunctive pattern: base form 'be' + V3 ('reconsidered') — 'is reconsidered' or 'was reconsidered' would be incorrect here.
